info.title: Zine REST API
info.description: REST API for reading and managing the Zine home dashboard, inbox items, bookmarks, creators, tags, article content, progress, subscriptions across supported sources, and sync jobs. Requests may authenticate with a Clerk session token or a scoped Zine personal access token.

tags.name: Home
tags.description: Read the ordered, personalized dashboard shelves.

tags.name: Inbox
tags.description: Read and triage unbookmarked inbox items.

tags.name: Bookmarks
tags.description: Read, save, update, and remove bookmarked items.

tags.name: Creators
tags.description: Read creator profiles, bookmarked content, and provider-backed latest content.

tags.name: Editorial
tags.description: Run, publish, read, and tune the daily X-led editorial.

tags.name: Reading
tags.description: Article content, opened events, and reading/playback progress.

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: Manage provider subscriptions. The current REST surface is scoped to YouTube.

tags.name: Sync
tags.description: Trigger and inspect asynchronous subscription sync jobs.

200.description: OpenAPI document for the Zine REST API.

get.description: Returns all content the authenticated user has bookmarked from this creator, newest bookmark first.

parameters.name: isFinished
parameters.description: Filter creator bookmarks by completion state. Omit to return all bookmarks.

get.description: Returns recent content from the creator's provider or discoverable feed when that source is supported.

200.description: Latest provider-backed content, or a reason it is unavailable.

429.description: The provider is temporarily rate limited.

get.description: Returns recently opened and saved bookmarks, content-type shelves, custom collections, and the user's configured section order.
Returns unfinished items currently in the inbox, sorted by most recently ingested first.

post.summary: Move an inbox item to bookmarks
post.description: REST equivalent of tRPC items.bookmark for inbox triage clients.
REST equivalent of tRPC items.archive for dismissing inbox items.

get.description: Returns bookmarked items, sorted by most recently bookmarked first. Supports provider and contentType filters to match tRPC items.library.

parameters.name: isFinished
parameters.description: When true, returns finished bookmarks. When false or omitted, returns unfinished bookmarks.

post.summary: Save a URL as a bookmark
post.description: Fetches a preview for the URL, creates or reuses the canonical item, and saves it as a bookmark for the token owner.

200.description: Bookmark saved, restored, or already present.

get.description: Returns unfinished bookmarked items the user has opened, sorted by most recently opened first.
Returns unfinished bookmarks that take ten minutes or less, sorted by most recently saved first.
Returns all bookmarks in a custom collection using the collection's configured sort order.

post.description: Read-only REST equivalent of tRPC bookmarks.preview for clients that want confirmation UI before saving.

get.description: REST equivalent of tRPC items.get for a single bookmarked item.

patch.summary: Mark a bookmark as finished or unfinished
patch.description: Sets the finished state for a bookmarked item; idempotent when the requested state already matches the stored state.

200.description: Bookmark finished state updated or already matched.

delete.description: REST equivalent of tRPC items.unbookmark. Archives the user item and clears bookmarkedAt.

post.summary: Record that a bookmark was opened
post.description: REST equivalent of tRPC items.markOpened for detail views and external readers.

get.description: Returns the current immutable article-body artifact when available, otherwise legacy stored HTML. Availability metadata distinguishes pending, degraded, unavailable, and legacy content.

200.description: Article content with explicit availability and provenance.

post.description: Idempotently requests article-body acquisition for an article the token owner can access. Current and terminal results are returned without duplicate queue work.

200.description: The article already has a current or terminal result.

202.description: Article acquisition was queued or is already processing.

422.description: The item is not an article.

get.summary: Get the current native Today issue
get.description: Returns the latest issue, expected local edition date, generation/freshness state, and source presentation map. Returns 200 with a null issue before the first publication.

200.description: Current or most recent Today state.
Editorial experiments and their current workflow state.

post.summary: Create an editable editorial experiment brief

get.summary: Get one experiment, variants, review, and exact next action

post.summary: Lock the experiment brief before variants are generated

200.description: Experiment marked failed with its diagnostic retained.

post.summary: Abandon an experiment without deleting its history

200.description: Experiment abandoned with all artifacts retained.

post.summary: Retain an immutable validated variant bundle for phone review

201.description: Variant retained outside the Today index.

get.summary: Get a variant through the native Today read model

200.description: Experiment metadata plus an EditorialTodayResponse preview.

post.summary: Record an idempotent A, B, or Neither phone review

201.description: Review recorded; no edition was published.

post.summary: Publish only the explicitly reviewed winning variant

200.description: Winning variant promoted or confirmed idempotently.

post.summary: Start an inspectable editorial generation run

409.description: Run ID already contains different metadata.

post.summary: Record a terminal editorial generation failure

200.description: Failure recorded or identical failure already existed.

409.description: Run is published or already contains a different failure.

get.summary: Get the bounded editorial tuning profile
get.description: Aggregates the last 180 days of explicit More like this, Less like this, Dismissed, and Already knew signals into normalized topic, creator, URL, and source preferences. Raw content is not returned.

200.description: Versioned editions for the token owner.

post.summary: Publish a validated daily editorial edition

409.description: Edition ID already contains different content.

get.summary: Get the latest daily editorial edition
List subscription sources and status summaries

200.description: YouTube, Spotify, Gmail, X, and RSS source summaries.

get.summary: List current and available Spotify shows

200.description: Spotify connection and show subscription state.

patch.summary: Pause or resume a Spotify show

200.description: Gmail connected and newsletter discovery started.
Gmail connection, newsletter list, and statistics.
X connection, imported count, and sync state.

patch.summary: Enable or disable daily X bookmark sync
Pause or resume an RSS feed

post.summary: Register a YouTube OAuth state value
post.description: Stores a one-time CSRF state value before the native client opens Google authorization.
Validates state, exchanges the authorization code using PKCE, and stores the encrypted provider connection.

delete.description: Revokes the provider token when possible, removes the encrypted connection, and marks YouTube subscriptions disconnected.

get.summary: List current and available YouTube channels
get.description: Returns Zine's current YouTube subscriptions together with followed YouTube channels that can be added.

patch.summary: Pause or resume a YouTube subscription

delete.description: Stops future ingestion, removes subscription-created inbox items, and preserves bookmarks.

post.description: REST endpoint for the existing asynchronous subscription sync process. The current implementation syncs active YouTube and Spotify subscriptions; source filtering and all-provider inbox refresh orchestration remain future work.

202.description: Sync job accepted, or an existing active job was returned.

get.description: REST endpoint returning the token owner's active subscription sync job when one is still in progress.
REST endpoint returning status for a sync job owned by the token owner.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Sync job ID returned by POST /api/v1/sync-jobs.

bearerAuth.bearerFormat: Clerk session JWT or Zine personal access token
bearerAuth.description: Use a Clerk session JWT for a signed-in Zine user, or a Zine personal access token with the required operation scope.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: Maximum number of items to return. Defaults to 10.

Cursor.name: cursor
Cursor.description: Cursor returned by the previous page.

CreatorId.name: creatorId
CreatorId.description: Stable creator ID attached to an item.

ValidationError.description: Invalid request body or query parameters.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, malformed, expired, revoked, or unknown token.

Forbidden.description: Token is missing the required scope.

NotFound.description: Resource not found for this token owner.

UnsupportedUrl.description: URL could not be previewed or is unsupported.

RateLimited.description: A sync job was started too recently.

EditorialEdition.description: Canonical versioned DailyEdition document defined by @zine/editorial-schema.

issues.description: Zod validation issues for the request.

itemId.description: Canonical item ID shared across users.

nextCursor.description: Cursor to pass to the next request. Null means there are no more items.

url.description: HTTP or HTTPS URL to save.

tags.description: Optional tag names to add to the saved bookmark.

url.description: HTTP or HTTPS URL to preview.

UpdateBookmarkFinishedStateRequest.description: Provide exactly one alias. All aliases set the same bookmark finished state.

CreateSyncJobRequest.description: Reserved for future sync options. Omit the body or send an empty object in the current API.

retryAfterSeconds.description: Seconds to wait before retrying, when known.

SyncTelemetry.description: Optional sync telemetry fields emitted by the worker.

total.description: Number of subscriptions queued for the job.

existing.description: True when an active job already existed and was returned.
